enrichment.short_description: Taskwarrior is a free, open-source command-line tool for managing task lists, offering powerful filtering, tagging, and reporting for developers and power users who prefer working in a terminal.
enrichment.seo_title: Taskwarrior Review: Free Command-Line Task Manager
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Taskwarrior is a free, open-source command-line task manager with tagging, filtering, and custom reports. See features, pros, cons, and FAQs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Taskwarrior is a free and open-source command-line task management tool that has been in active development since 2006, currently maintained by Gothenburg Bit Factory under the MIT license. It is designed for developers, sysadmins, and other terminal-native users who want to track a task list without leaving the command line. Core capabilities include quick task entry and modification, a flexible tagging system, priority levels, due dates and recurring tasks, projects and subprojects, custom and colorized reports, user-defined attributes, hook scripts for automation, and synchronization across machines via Taskserver. Companion tools in the same ecosystem include Timewarrior for time tracking and Taskshell for an interactive shell. Because Taskwarrior is entirely open source, there is no subscription or license fee; the project is funded through GitHub Sponsors and volunteer contributions rather than venture capital or a company. It runs on Linux, macOS, BSD, and other Unix-like systems, and is available through most package managers. Taskwarrior appeals to a niche but dedicated audience of power users who value speed, scriptability, plain-text data ownership, and deep customization over the visual polish of mainstream task apps like Todoist or Things. As of the current 3.x release series, it remains one of the most established and actively maintained command-line task managers available.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Taskwarrior
paragraphs: Taskwarrior is a free and open-source command-line application for managing a task list. First released in 2006 and now maintained by Gothenburg Bit Factory, it is built for people who prefer working entirely inside a terminal rather than a browser or mobile app.

The tool is intentionally minimal and fast, focused on letting users add, modify, complete, and query tasks with short commands. Data is stored locally, and the project ships companion tools such as Timewarrior for time tracking and Taskshell for an interactive shell experience.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Taskwarrior supports flexible tagging, priority levels, due dates, recurring tasks, and hierarchical projects and subprojects. Its reporting engine lets users build fully custom, colorized reports and filter tasks using sophisticated query expressions.
Advanced users extend Taskwarrior with hook scripts that run custom code on events like adding or completing a task, and with Taskserver for syncing a task list across multiple machines.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Taskwarrior is completely free and open source under the MIT license, with no paid tiers or subscriptions. Development is funded through voluntary contributions and GitHub Sponsors rather than commercial licensing.

features.title: Command-Line Interface
features.description: Fast task entry, modification, and completion using simple terminal commands.

features.title: Tagging and Filtering
features.description: Flexible tags and complex filter expressions to slice through large task lists.

features.title: Priorities and Due Dates
features.description: Assign urgency, due dates, and recurring schedules to keep work on track.

features.title: Projects and Subprojects
features.description: Organize tasks hierarchically across projects and subprojects.

features.title: Custom Reports
features.description: Build tailored report layouts and color rules for how tasks are displayed.

features.title: Hook Scripts
features.description: Trigger external scripts on task events for automation and integrations.

features.title: Synchronization
features.description: Sync tasks across devices using Taskserver.

features.title: Companion Tools
features.description: Timewarrior for time tracking and Taskshell for an interactive shell experience.

pros: Completely free and open source with no vendor lock-in
Extremely fast and lightweight, ideal for terminal workflows
Highly customizable filtering, reports, and automation via hook scripts
Full control over locally stored task data
Active community and long development history since 2006
cons: Steep learning curve for users unfamiliar with command-line tools
No official polished mobile app (relies on third-party clients)
No built-in team collaboration or shared workspaces
Minimal visual interface compared to mainstream task apps
Setup and syncing require some technical configuration

faqs.question: Is Taskwarrior free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Taskwarrior is completely free and open source under the MIT license.

faqs.question: What platforms does Taskwarrior run on?
faqs.answer: Linux, macOS, BSD, and other Unix-like systems; Windows users typically run it via WSL.

faqs.question: Does Taskwarrior have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: There is no official mobile app, but community-built clients exist for some platforms.

faqs.question: How do I sync tasks across devices?
faqs.answer: Taskwarrior supports synchronization through Taskserver, a self-hosted or third-party sync service.

faqs.question: Who maintains Taskwarrior?
faqs.answer: The project is maintained by Gothenburg Bit Factory and a community of open-source contributors.

faqs.question: Can I automate tasks in Taskwarrior?
faqs.answer: Yes, hook scripts let you trigger custom code on events like adding or completing a task.

faqs.question: Is there a graphical interface for Taskwarrior?
faqs.answer: Taskwarrior itself is command-line only, though third-party GUIs and terminal UIs built on top of it exist.

faqs.question: What is Timewarrior?
faqs.answer: Timewarrior is a companion open-source time-tracking tool that integrates with Taskwarrior.

use_cases.title: Developer Task Tracking
use_cases.description: Developers who live in the terminal use Taskwarrior to track coding tasks and bugs without switching context to a browser or app.

use_cases.title: Personal Productivity for Power Users
use_cases.description: Individuals who prefer plain-text, scriptable tools use Taskwarrior to manage personal task lists with custom tags, priorities, and reports.

use_cases.title: Automated Workflow Tracking
use_cases.description: Teams and individuals use Taskwarrior hook scripts to integrate task management into automated pipelines and personal automation setups.

features: Open source under the MIT license

enrichment.short_description: Tasmota is a free, open-source alternative firmware for ESP8266 and ESP32 based smart devices that replaces cloud-dependent stock firmware with fully local control over MQTT, HTTP, Serial, and a web UI.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tasmota is free, open-source firmware for ESP8266 and ESP32 smart devices with full local control. See features, pros, cons, and FAQs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Tasmota is a free, open-source firmware project for ESP8266, ESP32, ESP32-S, and ESP32-C3 based smart home devices, created by Theo Arends starting in January 2016 as a fork of the stock Sonoff firmware, originally named Sonoff-MQTT-OTA. It replaces manufacturer cloud firmware on smart plugs, switches, sensors, lighting, curtain motors, and other IoT hardware with fully local control, removing dependency on vendor cloud servers. Tasmota devices can be managed through a web UI, MQTT, HTTP, Serial, or KNX, and support automation via timers, rules, or Berry scripting. The project supports well over 2,000 device profiles out of the box and integrates with home automation platforms such as Home Assistant and openHAB. Installation is typically done via flashing tools such as Tasmotizer or the browser-based Tasmota WebInstaller. Tasmota has no company behind it and no pricing tiers; it is maintained by an open-source community on GitHub and distributed under an open-source license. It is popular among smart home hobbyists and privacy-conscious users who want to keep IoT devices working entirely on a local network, independent of a vendor's cloud availability, and is commonly compared to ESPHome as a leading open-source alternative firmware option.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Tasmota
paragraphs: Tasmota is open-source firmware for ESP8266 and ESP32 based smart devices, created by Theo Arends beginning in January 2016. It was originally built to replace the stock cloud-dependent firmware on Sonoff smart plugs and switches with a version that runs entirely on the local network.

Today Tasmota supports thousands of devices from many manufacturers, including switches, sensors, lighting, and curtain motors, and is maintained by an active open-source community on GitHub rather than a company.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Tasmota devices can be controlled and configured through a built-in web UI, MQTT, HTTP, Serial, or KNX, without any dependency on a manufacturer's cloud service. Automation is handled through built-in timers and rules or more advanced Berry scripting.

The firmware integrates with popular home automation hubs such as Home Assistant and openHAB, and can typically be installed using the browser-based Tasmota WebInstaller without needing to open the device.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Tasmota is completely free and open source, with no licensing fees, subscriptions, or paid tiers. It is distributed and maintained purely through community contribution.

features.title: Local-Only Control
features.description: Operate devices entirely over the local network without any cloud dependency.

features.title: Broad Device Support
features.description: Configuration templates for thousands of ESP8266 and ESP32 based devices.

features.title: Multiple Protocols
features.description: Control devices via web UI, MQTT, HTTP, Serial, or KNX.

features.title: Automation Rules and Timers
features.description: Built-in rule engine plus Berry scripting for custom automation logic.

features.title: Over-the-Air Updates
features.description: Flash and update firmware without physical access after initial setup.

features.title: Home Automation Integrations
features.description: Works with Home Assistant, openHAB, and other MQTT-based hubs.

features.title: Web-Based Installer
features.description: Flash firmware directly from a browser using the Tasmota WebInstaller.

features.title: Active Open-Source Community
features.description: Frequent releases and community-contributed device templates.

pros: Completely free, open-source, no subscription or cloud account required
Keeps smart home devices working without an internet connection
Supports thousands of ESP8266 and ESP32 based devices out of the box
Deep automation via built-in rules and Berry scripting
Large, active community with frequent updates and device templates
cons: Requires flashing firmware, which carries some technical risk
Some manufacturers lock devices, making flashing difficult or voiding warranties
No official customer support channel, only community-based support
User interface is functional but less polished than commercial smart home apps
Not every device or sensor on the market is supported

faqs.question: Is Tasmota free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Tasmota is completely free, open-source firmware.

faqs.question: What hardware does Tasmota support?
faqs.answer: ESP8266, ESP32, ESP32-S, and ESP32-C3 based smart devices, with templates for thousands of specific products.

faqs.question: Do I need the cloud to use Tasmota?
faqs.answer: No, Tasmota is designed for entirely local control over MQTT, HTTP, Serial, or a local web UI.

faqs.question: How do I install Tasmota on a device?
faqs.answer: Most users flash devices using the browser-based Tasmota WebInstaller or a tool such as Tasmotizer.

faqs.question: Does Tasmota work with Home Assistant?
faqs.answer: Yes, Tasmota integrates with Home Assistant and other MQTT-based home automation platforms.

faqs.question: Who created Tasmota?
faqs.answer: Tasmota was created by Theo Arends, starting in January 2016 as a fork of Sonoff's stock firmware.

faqs.question: Is flashing Tasmota reversible?
faqs.answer: In most cases devices can be reflashed back to stock firmware, though this varies by device and is not guaranteed.

faqs.question: How is Tasmota different from ESPHome?
faqs.answer: Both are open-source alternative firmware projects for ESP chips; Tasmota emphasizes a built-in web UI and rule engine, while ESPHome emphasizes YAML-based configuration tightly integrated with Home Assistant.

use_cases.title: Privacy-Focused Smart Home Setup
use_cases.description: Homeowners flash Tasmota onto smart plugs and switches to keep device control entirely on their local network, avoiding manufacturer cloud services.

use_cases.title: Custom IoT Automation
use_cases.description: Makers and hobbyists use Tasmota's rule engine and Berry scripting to build custom automations on top of low-cost ESP-based hardware.

use_cases.title: Extending Legacy or Cloud-Locked Devices
use_cases.description: Users replace discontinued or cloud-dependent stock firmware on devices such as Sonoff switches with Tasmota to keep them functional long-term.

features: Open-source firmware for ESP8266 and ESP32 devices
Full local control with no cloud requirement

enrichment.short_description: TaxJar is a sales tax automation platform, now part of Stripe, that helps e-commerce and SaaS businesses calculate, report, and file sales tax across US jurisdictions.
enrichment.company_size: 200+ employees at the time of its 2021 acquisition by Stripe
enrichment.seo_title: TaxJar Review: Sales Tax Automation by Stripe
enrichment.seo_meta_description: TaxJar automates US sales tax calculation, nexus tracking, and filing. See TaxJar pricing, features, pros, cons, and FAQs from Stripe's tax platform.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: TaxJar is a cloud-based sales tax compliance platform founded in 2013 to help e-commerce and SaaS businesses automate US sales tax calculation, nexus tracking, reporting, and filing. Stripe acquired TaxJar in 2021 for roughly $850-900 million, and TaxJar now operates as part of Stripe's revenue and tax compliance infrastructure while continuing to run as a standalone product. TaxJar connects to platforms such as Shopify, Amazon, WooCommerce, and Stripe itself to pull order data, calculate accurate rates at checkout, track when a business crosses economic nexus thresholds in a state, generate jurisdiction-level reports, and automatically file and remit returns through its AutoFile feature. Pricing starts with a Starter plan at $39 per month for up to 200 orders and includes a Professional plan at $99 per month with phone support, more AutoFile credits, and a dedicated Customer Success Manager; both plans offer a 30-day free trial and a discount for annual billing. TaxJar is aimed at online sellers, marketplaces, and finance teams that need to stay compliant with sales tax rules across thousands of US tax jurisdictions without manually tracking rate changes or filing deadlines in each state.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is TaxJar
paragraphs: TaxJar is a sales tax compliance platform founded in 2013 to help internet businesses automate sales tax calculation, nexus tracking, reporting, and filing across the United States. In 2021, Stripe acquired TaxJar to build out its tax compliance infrastructure.

TaxJar connects to e-commerce and payment platforms to pull order data automatically, then applies jurisdiction-accurate tax rates and tracks when a business needs to register in a new state based on economic nexus rules.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core features include real-time sales tax calculation, an economic nexus dashboard with alerts, jurisdiction-level reporting, and a developer API. Its AutoFile feature automatically files and remits sales tax returns in supported states, reducing manual filing work.
TaxJar also offers audit support and notice management, along with CSV import options and integrations with major sales channels like Shopify, Amazon, and WooCommerce.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: TaxJar offers two published plans: Starter at $39/month and Professional at $99/month, both with a 30-day free trial and a discount for annual billing. Additional AutoFile credits and Flex Fees for temporary volume spikes are available beyond the included allotments.

features.title: Sales Tax Calculation
features.description: Real-time, jurisdiction-accurate tax rates applied at checkout.

features.title: Economic Nexus Tracking
features.description: Dashboard and alerts for when a business crosses state nexus thresholds.

features.title: AutoFile
features.description: Automated filing and remittance of sales tax returns in supported states.

features.title: Jurisdiction-Level Reporting
features.description: Detailed reports broken down by state, county, and local jurisdiction.

features.title: Platform Integrations
features.description: Connects with Shopify, Amazon, WooCommerce, Stripe, and other sales channels.

features.title: Developer API
features.description: REST API for custom sales tax calculation integrations.

features.title: Audit Support
features.description: Notice management and support during state tax audits.

features.title: CSV and Data Import
features.description: Simple and advanced import options for order and sales data.

pros: Backed by Stripe's infrastructure and reliability after the 2021 acquisition
Automates filing and remittance in states with AutoFile, saving significant manual work
Broad integration coverage with major e-commerce and payment platforms
Clear nexus tracking helps businesses avoid unexpected tax exposure
Transparent, published pricing with a 30-day free trial
cons: Focused on US sales tax only, not international VAT or GST
AutoFile credits are limited per plan; extra filings cost more
Higher-volume sellers may need the pricier Professional plan for adequate support
Setup and nexus configuration can be complex for multi-state sellers
Integration limits on the Starter tier compared to Professional

faqs.question: Is TaxJar owned by Stripe?
faqs.answer: Yes, Stripe acquired TaxJar in 2021, and it now operates as part of Stripe's tax compliance offerings.

faqs.question: How much does TaxJar cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $39/month for the Starter tier and $99/month for the Professional tier, with a discount for annual billing.

faqs.question: Does TaxJar file sales tax returns automatically?
faqs.answer: Yes, through its AutoFile feature, TaxJar can automatically file and remit sales tax returns in supported states using included AutoFile credits.

faqs.question: What platforms does TaxJar integrate with?
faqs.answer: TaxJar integrates with Shopify, Amazon, WooCommerce, Stripe, and other major sales and payment platforms.

faqs.question: Does TaxJar handle international tax?
faqs.answer: TaxJar's core product focuses on US sales tax across state and local jurisdictions.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, both TaxJar plans offer a 30-day free trial.

faqs.question: What is economic nexus?
faqs.answer: It is the sales or transaction threshold that determines when a business must start collecting sales tax in a given state; TaxJar tracks this automatically.

faqs.question: Can TaxJar help with a state tax audit?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Professional plan includes audit support and notice management.

use_cases.title: E-Commerce Sales Tax Compliance
use_cases.description: Online stores use TaxJar to calculate accurate sales tax at checkout across every US state and automatically file returns as they grow.
use_cases.audience: E-commerce businesses and Shopify or WooCommerce sellers

use_cases.title: Multi-State Nexus Monitoring
use_cases.description: Growing businesses use TaxJar's nexus dashboard to know exactly when they cross a sales threshold that requires tax registration in a new state.

use_cases.title: SaaS and Marketplace Tax Automation
use_cases.description: SaaS companies and marketplaces integrate TaxJar's API to calculate tax programmatically at the point of sale.

features: Email support with guaranteed response time
Up to 10 total integrations (data import plus API)
Email and phone support with guaranteed response time
Dedicated onboarding and Customer Success Manager

enrichment.short_description: Teable is an open-source, PostgreSQL-native no-code database that gives teams an Airtable-like spreadsheet interface backed by real database power, with self-hosted and cloud options.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Teable is an open-source, PostgreSQL-native no-code database. See Teable pricing, features, pros, cons, and FAQs versus Airtable.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Teable is an open-source, no-code database platform built directly on PostgreSQL, positioned as a next-generation Airtable alternative. Launched around 2023, it combines a familiar spreadsheet-style interface with a real relational database underneath, offering Grid, Kanban, Calendar, Gallery, and Form views alongside real-time collaboration. Teable supports AI-powered fields that auto-populate data, an integrated AI chat for querying the database, and an App Builder for creating internal tools without code. Because it runs on native PostgreSQL, Teable is built to scale to large datasets, with its Business plan supporting up to 1,000,000 rows per space and unlimited rows per table, well beyond what typical spreadsheet-based no-code tools handle. The Community Edition is free and self-hostable under the AGPL license, while Teable also offers a hosted cloud service with a Free tier and paid Pro ($10/seat/month billed yearly) and Business ($20/seat/month billed yearly) plans that add features like SSO, custom domains, and an authority matrix for granular permissions. The project has attracted over 21,000 GitHub stars and backing from investors including Baidu Ventures, Vertex Ventures China, and ZhenFund. Teable targets startups, operations teams, and developers who want Airtable-style flexibility without giving up SQL-grade performance or the option to self-host.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Teable
paragraphs: Teable is an open-source, no-code database built natively on PostgreSQL, launched around 2023 as a next-generation alternative to spreadsheet-style database tools like Airtable.

It presents a familiar spreadsheet interface on top of a real relational database, letting teams get the ease of a no-code tool without the row and performance limits typical of spreadsheet-based platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Teable supports Grid, Kanban, Calendar, Gallery, and Form views on the same underlying data, along with real-time collaboration for teams working together. AI-powered fields and an integrated AI chat let users auto-populate data and query the database in natural language.

An App Builder allows teams to create custom internal tools without writing code, and because Teable is PostgreSQL-native it can handle very large datasets, up to 1,000,000 rows per space on the Business plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Teable offers a free, self-hostable Community Edition under the AGPL license, plus a hosted cloud service with a Free tier and paid Pro ($10/seat/month billed yearly, $12/month billed monthly) and Business ($20/seat/month billed yearly, $24/month billed monthly) plans that add SSO, custom domains, and higher usage limits.

features.title: PostgreSQL-Native Architecture
features.description: A real relational database underneath a familiar spreadsheet UI.

features.title: Multiple Views
features.description: Grid, Kanban, Calendar, Gallery, and Form views on the same data.

features.title: Real-Time Collaboration
features.description: Multiple users editing and viewing data simultaneously.

features.title: AI-Powered Fields and Chat
features.description: Auto-populate fields and query the database using AI.

features.title: App Builder
features.description: Build custom internal tools and workflows without code.

features.title: Self-Hosting Option
features.description: Free, open-source Community Edition under the AGPL license.

features.title: High Row Capacity
features.description: Supports up to 1,000,000 rows per space on the Business plan.

features.title: Automation Runs
features.description: Built-in automation with per-plan run limits.

pros: Free, open-source self-hosted edition with no artificial row limits
Handles much larger datasets than typical spreadsheet-based no-code tools
Familiar Airtable-like interface lowers the learning curve
Transparent per-seat cloud pricing with a usable free tier
Active open-source community with rapid GitHub star growth
cons: Younger product with a shorter track record than established competitors
AI features consume metered credits that can run out on lower tiers
Self-hosting requires technical setup and PostgreSQL familiarity
Enterprise-grade features like SSO are limited to the higher-priced Business plan
Smaller integration and template ecosystem compared to Airtable

faqs.question: Is Teable free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Teable offers a free self-hosted Community Edition under the AGPL license and a free tier on its hosted cloud service.

faqs.question: How much does Teable Pro cost?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan starts at $10/seat/month billed yearly, or $12/month billed monthly.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Teable?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition can be self-hosted for free since it is open source.

faqs.question: Is Teable built on a real database?
faqs.answer: Yes, Teable is built natively on PostgreSQL rather than a proprietary spreadsheet engine.

faqs.question: How many rows can Teable handle?
faqs.answer: The Business plan supports up to 1,000,000 rows per space, with unlimited rows per table.

faqs.question: Does Teable have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Teable offers AI-powered fields and an integrated AI chat for querying data, using a metered credit system.

faqs.question: Does Teable support single sign-on?
faqs.answer: SSO and domain verification are available on the Business plan.

faqs.question: Who is behind Teable?
faqs.answer: Teable is developed by an open-source team and backed by investors including Baidu Ventures, Vertex Ventures China, and ZhenFund.

use_cases.title: Self-Hosted Internal Database
use_cases.description: Engineering and ops teams self-host Teable's Community Edition to build internal tools and databases without relying on a third-party cloud service.

use_cases.title: No-Code App Building
use_cases.description: Startups use Teable's App Builder and multiple views to create lightweight internal apps and dashboards without writing code.

use_cases.title: Large-Scale Data Management
use_cases.description: Teams outgrowing spreadsheet-based tools move to Teable to manage large datasets with real database performance while keeping a spreadsheet-style interface.
use_cases.audience: Data and operations teams handling large datasets

features: AI chat, AI field, and app builder included
Custom app domain, authority matrix, domain verification, and SSO
Open source under the AGPL license

enrichment.short_description: Teachable is an online course creation platform that lets creators and businesses build, market, and sell courses, coaching, and digital products, now part of the Hotmart Company.
enrichment.company_size: Approximately 300-340 employees (2026, as part of Hotmart Company)
enrichment.headquarters: New York, New York, United States
enrichment.seo_title: Teachable Review: Online Course Platform Pricing
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Teachable lets creators sell online courses and digital products. See Teachable pricing, features, pros, cons, and FAQs for 2026.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Teachable is an online course and digital product platform founded in 2014 by Ankur Nagpal, building on an earlier 2013 project called Fedora, and headquartered in New York City. It lets creators, coaches, and businesses build branded course sites and sell video courses, coaching packages, digital downloads, and communities without needing separate hosting or payment infrastructure. Teachable was acquired by the Brazilian creator-commerce company Hotmart in 2020 and now operates as part of the larger Hotmart Company group. Its creator plans run from Starter at $39/month (or $29/month billed annually) with a 7.5% transaction fee, up to Builder ($89/month), Growth ($189/month), and a Custom tier for businesses selling over $60,000 per year; Builder and above remove Teachable's platform transaction fee. Teachable also offers Enterprise plans (Core and Pro) priced from $6,000 to $12,000 per year for schools and larger organizations that need SSO, white-labeling, and dedicated account management. Key features include an AI-assisted course builder, iOS and Android student apps, global payment support, certificates, affiliate marketing tools, and bulk student imports on higher tiers. Teachable has powered over 100,000 creators and billions of dollars in course sales, competing with platforms like Kajabi, Thinkific, and Podia in the creator economy and online education space.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Teachable
paragraphs: Teachable is an online course and digital product platform founded in 2014 by Ankur Nagpal, building on an earlier 2013 project called Fedora, and headquartered in New York City. It was acquired by the Brazilian creator-commerce company Hotmart in 2020 and now operates as part of the Hotmart Company group.

The platform lets creators, coaches, and businesses build a branded course site and sell video courses, coaching packages, digital downloads, and communities without needing separate hosting, payment, or student-management infrastructure.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Teachable includes an AI-assisted course builder, global payment support, dedicated iOS and Android apps for students, completion certificates, and affiliate marketing tools on higher plans. Growth-tier customers get bulk student imports, custom permissions, and free subtitles and translations.
Enterprise customers get single sign-on, white-labeling, and dedicated account management, positioning Teachable for both individual creators and larger training organizations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Creator plans range from Starter at $39/month ($29/month billed annually) up to Growth at $189/month, with a Custom tier for larger sellers. Enterprise plans start at $6,000/year for Core and $12,000/year for Pro. Starter charges a 7.5% transaction fee, while Builder and above have 0% platform transaction fees.

features.title: Course and Product Builder
features.description: Build video courses, coaching offers, and digital downloads with AI-assisted content tools.

features.title: Global Payments
features.description: Accept payments from students worldwide with built-in checkout.

features.title: Mobile Apps
features.description: Dedicated iOS and Android apps for students to access courses.

features.title: Affiliate Marketing
features.description: Built-in affiliate program tools on Builder plan and above.

features.title: Course Certificates
features.description: Issue completion certificates to students automatically.

features.title: Custom Permissions and Bulk Imports
features.description: Manage larger teams and student rosters on the Growth plan.

features.title: White-Labeling
features.description: Remove Teachable branding on paid plans.

features.title: Enterprise SSO
features.description: Single sign-on and dedicated account management on Enterprise tiers.

pros: No coding required to launch a fully branded course site
Backed by Hotmart's global creator-commerce infrastructure since 2020
0% platform transaction fees on Builder plan and above
Strong track record with over 100,000 creators and billions in course sales
Mobile apps give students a native way to consume course content
cons: Starter plan charges a 7.5% transaction fee on every sale
Pricing rises quickly for creators who need multiple admin users or high student caps
Enterprise pricing requires a significant annual commitment starting at $6,000
Less design flexibility than fully custom-built websites
Payment processing fees apply on top of subscription costs

faqs.question: How much does Teachable cost?
faqs.answer: Creator plans start at $39/month (or $29/month billed annually) for Starter, up to $189/month for Growth, plus a Custom tier for larger businesses.

faqs.question: Does Teachable charge transaction fees?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan charges a 7.5% transaction fee; Builder and higher plans have 0% platform transaction fees, though payment processing fees still apply.

faqs.question: Who owns Teachable?
faqs.answer: Teachable is owned by the Hotmart Company, which acquired it in 2020.

faqs.question: Where is Teachable based?
faqs.answer: Teachable is headquartered in New York, New York.

faqs.question: Does Teachable have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Teachable offers dedicated iOS and Android apps for students.

faqs.question: Can I sell coaching or digital downloads, not just courses?
faqs.answer: Yes, Teachable supports courses, coaching, communities, and digital downloads as sellable products.

faqs.question: Does Teachable offer an Enterprise plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Enterprise plans start at $6,000/year (Core) and $12,000/year (Pro), with custom pricing for larger organizations.

faqs.question: When was Teachable founded?
faqs.answer: Teachable was founded in 2014 by Ankur Nagpal, building on an earlier 2013 platform called Fedora.

use_cases.title: Independent Creator Course Sales
use_cases.description: Solo creators and coaches use Teachable to package expertise into paid video courses and coaching offers without building their own website infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Teamgate is a cloud-based sales CRM built for small and mid-sized sales teams, offering pipeline management, sales analytics, and workflow automation.
enrichment.seo_title: Teamgate CRM Review: Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Teamgate is a cloud sales CRM for small and mid-sized teams. See Teamgate pricing, features, pros, cons, and FAQs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Teamgate is a cloud-based sales CRM founded in 2012 in Vilnius, Lithuania, originally under the name Synergy Cloud, with an additional office in London, UK. It is built for small and mid-sized sales teams and SaaS companies that want pipeline management, contact and deal tracking, sales analytics, and workflow automation without the complexity of enterprise CRM suites. Teamgate's free Starter plan supports up to two users with basic pipeline tools and 500 contacts, while paid tiers add unlimited contacts, email sync, in-app calling and SMS, sales dashboards, lead scoring, and an open API. The Team plan is priced at $39.90 per user per month and the Growth plan at $59.90 per user per month, both billed monthly with roughly a 20% discount for annual billing; higher tiers add 24/7 support, dedicated account managers, and multi-currency support. Teamgate positions itself for sales-led growth companies, from early-stage startups to established SMBs, and remains a relatively small, independent company compared to CRM giants like Salesforce or HubSpot. It is commonly evaluated as a simpler, more affordable CRM alternative for teams that want strong pipeline visibility and sales reporting without a lengthy implementation process.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Teamgate
paragraphs: Teamgate is a cloud-based sales CRM founded in 2012 in Vilnius, Lithuania, originally under the name Synergy Cloud, with an additional office in London, UK. It is built for small and mid-sized sales teams that want pipeline visibility without the complexity of enterprise CRM suites.
The platform combines contact and deal tracking, sales analytics, and workflow automation in a single, relatively lightweight product aimed at sales-led growth companies.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Teamgate offers a visual, drag-and-drop sales pipeline, sales analytics dashboards, two-way email sync, and in-app calling and SMS. Higher tiers add lead scoring, workflow automation, an open API, and multi-currency support.
Paid plans also include dedicated account management and 24/7 support, giving smaller sales teams access to service levels typically reserved for larger CRM contracts.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Teamgate offers a free Starter plan for up to 2 users, a Team plan at $39.90 per user per month, and a Growth plan at $59.90 per user per month, both billed monthly with roughly a 20% discount for annual billing.

faqs.question: How much does Teamgate cost?
faqs.answer: The Team plan is $39.90 per user per month and the Growth plan is $59.90 per user per month, both with a discount for annual billing.

faqs.question: Where is Teamgate based?
faqs.answer: Teamgate is headquartered in Vilnius, Lithuania, with an additional office in London, UK.

faqs.question: When was Teamgate founded?
faqs.answer: Teamgate was founded in 2012, originally under the name Synergy Cloud.

faqs.question: Does Teamgate support calling and SMS?
faqs.answer: Yes, in-app dialing and SMS are available starting on the Team plan.

faqs.question: Does Teamgate have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Growth plan includes access to an Open API.

faqs.answer: Yes, Teamgate offers a free trial in addition to its free Starter plan.

faqs.answer: Teamgate is designed for small and mid-sized sales teams and SaaS companies seeking straightforward pipeline management.

enrichment.short_description: TeamSupport is a B2B customer support platform that helps technical support teams manage tickets, live chat, and account-level customer health with tools like its Customer Distress Index.
enrichment.company_size: 51-200 employees (LinkedIn); other estimates report around 66 employees as of 2024
enrichment.seo_title: TeamSupport Review: B2B Customer Support Software
enrichment.seo_meta_description: TeamSupport is B2B customer support software with account-level health tracking. See pricing, features, pros, cons, and FAQs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: TeamSupport is a B2B customer support software company founded in 2008 and headquartered in Dallas, Texas, with additional offices in Denver, Colorado, and Cape Town, South Africa. Unlike consumer-focused helpdesk tools, TeamSupport is purpose-built for business-to-business support teams that need to track relationships at the account level rather than just the individual ticket or user level. Its best-known feature is the Customer Distress Index (CDI), a proprietary account health score that blends product usage, sentiment, and support activity to flag at-risk customers before they churn. The platform includes ticketing, a customer portal, knowledge base, playbooks for automated workflows, and an AI assistant called Kevin AI. TeamSupport acquired live chat provider SnapEngage in 2021 to add messaging and live chat capabilities. Pricing starts at $45/month (billed annually) for the Starter plan, which supports up to 5 users, rising to $65/month for Professional (unlimited users) and $85/month for Scale (unlimited users, more playbooks, and analytics), with live chat and messaging available as a $15/agent/month add-on across all plans. TeamSupport is privately held and backed by growth investors including Level Equity, and is aimed at B2B software and technology companies whose support success depends on tracking whole-account health rather than isolated support interactions.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is TeamSupport
paragraphs: TeamSupport is a B2B customer support software company founded in 2008 and headquartered in Dallas, Texas, with additional offices in Denver and Cape Town. It is purpose-built for business-to-business support teams that need to track relationships at the account level rather than just the individual ticket.
Its best-known feature is the Customer Distress Index, a proprietary account health score that combines product usage, sentiment, and support activity to flag at-risk customers before they churn.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: TeamSupport includes ticketing, a customer portal, knowledge base, and playbooks for automated support workflows, along with a built-in AI assistant called Kevin AI. In 2021, TeamSupport acquired live chat provider SnapEngage to add messaging and live chat capabilities.
Higher-tier plans add more automation playbooks, custom fields, and an Insights analytics add-on for deeper reporting on support performance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: TeamSupport's published plans are Starter at $45/month (up to 5 users), Professional at $65/month, and Scale at $85/month, all billed annually with unlimited users on Professional and Scale. Live chat and messaging are available as a $15/agent/month add-on, and additional playbooks, analytics, and data import carry separate fees.

cons: Starter plan is capped at 5 users
Several features such as live chat, analytics, and extra playbooks are priced as separate add-ons
Pricing can add up quickly once add-ons are included
Smaller ecosystem and brand recognition than larger helpdesk platforms like Zendesk
Assisted data import and professional services carry extra hourly or per-record fees

faqs.question: How much does TeamSupport cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $45/month for Starter (billed annually, up to 5 users), $65/month for Professional, and $85/month for Scale (both with unlimited users).

faqs.question: What is the Customer Distress Index?
faqs.answer: It is TeamSupport's proprietary account health score that combines usage, sentiment, and support activity to flag at-risk B2B customers.

faqs.question: Is TeamSupport built for B2B or B2C support?
faqs.answer: TeamSupport is specifically designed for B2B customer support, tracking relationships at the account level.

faqs.question: Does TeamSupport include live chat?
faqs.answer: Live chat and messaging are available as a $15/agent/month add-on across all plans, via the SnapEngage acquisition.

faqs.question: Where is TeamSupport headquartered?
faqs.answer: TeamSupport is headquartered in Dallas, Texas, with offices in Denver and Cape Town.

faqs.question: When was TeamSupport founded?
faqs.answer: TeamSupport was founded in 2008.

faqs.question: Does TeamSupport have an AI assistant?
faqs.answer: Yes, TeamSupport includes an AI assistant called Kevin AI on Professional and Scale plans.

faqs.question: Are there additional fees beyond the subscription?
faqs.answer: Yes, add-ons like extra playbooks, broadcast messages, analytics, and assisted data import carry separate costs.

enrichment.short_description: Teamtailor is a Swedish applicant tracking system and employer branding platform that helps companies attract, manage, and hire candidates through career sites and recruitment workflows.
enrichment.starting_price: Custom pricing (reported to start around $229/month for small teams)
enrichment.company_size: 501-1,000 employees (LinkedIn); other estimates report 380-589 employees depending on source and date
enrichment.seo_title: Teamtailor Review: ATS and Employer Branding Software
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Teamtailor is an applicant tracking and employer branding platform from Stockholm. See features, pricing, pros, cons, and FAQs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Teamtailor is a recruitment software company founded in 2013 and headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden, focused on applicant tracking (ATS) combined with employer branding tools. More than 13,000 companies across over 90 countries use Teamtailor to build branded career sites, manage job postings, track candidates through hiring pipelines, and collaborate on interviews and offers. The platform includes over 250 features, such as unlimited job postings, unlimited users, video interviewing, referral tools, GDPR-compliant candidate data handling, and integrations with LinkedIn and other HR tech. Teamtailor does not publish fixed pricing tiers; instead it uses a custom, quote-based model that scales with company size and hiring volume rather than a flat per-seat fee, with reported starting costs around $229/month (roughly $2,750/year) for small teams and mid-market companies often paying $6,000 to $15,000 per year. It offers a 14-day free trial and separate go-to-market tracks for small-to-midsize businesses versus enterprise organizations with 1,000+ employees. Teamtailor has grown into a company with several hundred employees across multiple continents and reported roughly $41.8 million in annual recurring revenue as of 2025, competing with recruitment platforms like Greenhouse, Lever, and Workable in the ATS and employer branding space.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Teamtailor
paragraphs: Teamtailor is a recruitment software company founded in 2013 and headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden, combining applicant tracking with employer branding tools. More than 13,000 companies in over 90 countries use it to manage hiring.
The platform is built around the idea that recruiting is also a marketing problem, giving companies tools to build a branded career site alongside a traditional ATS workflow.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Teamtailor includes a career site builder, configurable candidate pipelines, video interviewing, employee referral tools, and GDPR-compliant candidate data handling. Every plan includes unlimited job postings and unlimited users.
The platform integrates with LinkedIn and other HR tech, and offers over 250 features across sourcing, screening, interviewing, and offer management.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Teamtailor does not publish fixed pricing tiers; costs are quote-based and scale with company size and hiring volume rather than a flat per-seat fee. Reported starting costs are around $229/month for small teams, with mid-market companies often paying $6,000 to $15,000 per year. A 14-day free trial is available.

Unlimited job postings and users included in every plan
Wide integration coverage across job boards and HR tech
Used by a large, established customer base of over 13,000 companies globally
14-day free trial available before committing to a quote
cons: No published pricing, requiring a sales conversation for any quote
Can be more expensive than lightweight ATS tools for very small teams
Pricing scales with company size, which can make costs less predictable
Feature depth of 250+ features can mean a learning curve for smaller teams
Primarily strongest for mid-market and enterprise hiring needs

faqs.question: How much does Teamtailor cost?
faqs.answer: Teamtailor uses custom, quote-based pricing that scales with company size, with reported starting costs around $229/month for small teams.

faqs.question: Where is Teamtailor headquartered?
faqs.answer: Teamtailor is headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden.

faqs.question: When was Teamtailor founded?
faqs.answer: Teamtailor was founded in 2013.

faqs.question: Does Teamtailor offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Teamtailor offers a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: Are there user limits on Teamtailor plans?
faqs.answer: No, Teamtailor includes unlimited job postings and unlimited users on its plans.

faqs.question: Does Teamtailor include employer branding tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, employer branding and career site building are core parts of the platform, not just applicant tracking.

faqs.question: How many companies use Teamtailor?
faqs.answer: Teamtailor is used by more than 13,000 companies in over 90 countries.

faqs.question: Does Teamtailor serve enterprise companies?
faqs.answer: Yes, Teamtailor has separate offerings for small-to-midsize businesses and enterprise organizations with 1,000+ employees.
